ogTitle: Barron Gorge vs Bartle Frere: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Barron Gorge and Bartle Frere.
heading: Barron Gorge vs Bartle Frere — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Barron Gorge, Queensland and Bartle Frere, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bartle Frere has a lower overall climate risk score (29/100) compared to Barron Gorge (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Barron Gorge or Bartle Frere safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Barron Gorge has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Bartle Frere scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Barron Gorge scores 53/100 for bushfire risk. Bartle Frere scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Barron Gorge or Bartle Frere?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bartle Frere has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
